sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 20.31 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.20)

statement.latex: Let$(G)$denote the set of element orders of a finite group$G$, and$h(G)$the number of pairwise nonisomorphic finite groups$H$with$(H)= (G)$. Find$h(L)$, where [r]{a)\;}$L$is the symmetric group of degree$10$; [r]{b)\;}$L$is the automorphism group of the simple sporadic Janko group$J_2$; [r]{c)\;}$PSL(2,q)<L (PSL(2,q))$. See the current status in (M.\,A.\,Grechkoseeva, V.\,D.\,Mazurov, W.\,J.\,Shi,...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
